identity.title: All Other Miscellaneous Chemical Product and Preparation Manufacturing

lens.included_activities: US LMM manufacturers that repeatedly formulate, blend, activate, package, or custom-produce miscellaneous chemical preparations for external customers, including activated carbon, water-treatment compounds, industrial process aids, lubricants and maintenance chemicals, and other recurring specialty formulations captured by 325998.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal plants, one-off R&D without repeat production, pure distribution, commodity resale without preparation, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat B2B or channel sales of consumable formulations and materials, often supported by technical service, qualification, application know-how, private-label arrangements, or recurring custom production; some branded maintenance products also sell through distribution.

a.bridge: BLS measures the occupation mix of all NAICS 325 chemical manufacturing, not 325998 or the LMM lens, and Eloundou et al. measure LLM exposure across US occupations rather than this industry's wage-weighted unautomated tasks. I use the BLS production share to limit exposure in physical batch, packaging, maintenance, and handling work, while assigning higher exposure to formulation support, purchasing, scheduling, quality records, regulatory documentation, customer service, sales, and administration. The bridge may understate small specialty firms' office burden or overstate exposure where plants already use automated batching and ERP workflows.
a.rationale: Broader chemical manufacturing is 39.02% production occupations, including 12.28% chemical-equipment operators, 6.43% packaging and filling operators, 3.57% mixing and blending operators, and 3.33% inspectors and testers. Much of that work is physical and safety constrained, while adjacent planning, document, commercial, technical-support, and inspection tasks are addressable by AI and machine vision. Economy-wide research finds 15% of tasks accelerable by LLM alone and 47%-56% with software, providing an outer context rather than an industry estimate.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted SOC distribution for 325998, paired with plant-level task inventories, current automation maps, and measured AI or vision pilots across batch production, quality, regulatory, sales, planning, and administration, would materially change the range.
caveats: The occupation evidence is three-digit NAICS 325 and not wage-weighted to 325998.
The code is a residual category spanning products with very different labor and automation intensity.
LLM task exposure is not the same as substitution and may include tasks already automated.

rho.rationale: Five-year implementation should be feasible for customer and sales support, purchasing, production and inventory planning, specification and SDS drafting, batch-record review, quality analytics, maintenance diagnostics, and selected machine-vision checks. It will be slower in control loops and release decisions because formulations are diverse, plants often have fragmented data and legacy equipment, and chemical operations require validation, cybersecurity, safety, environmental compliance, and human accountability. DOE documents application-specific algorithms, complex data acquisition, capital intensity, testing, scale-up, and disruption risk in chemical manufacturing.
rho.change_evidence: Documented before-and-after deployments at independent 325998 firms showing hours saved, avoided hires, exception rates, validation and integration costs, quality escapes, and payback by workflow would materially move the estimate.
caveats: DOE evidence covers chemical and thermal processing broadly rather than the 325998 residual category.
Implementation will differ sharply between simple blending and highly engineered activated-carbon or specialty-chemical production.

e.rationale: Most products in this category are consumed, depleted, or incorporated into customers' processes and therefore generate repeat demand. Public examples show activated carbon used as a consumable across water, gas, emissions, and remediation markets; maintenance chemicals also generate repeat sales. Eligibility is reduced for captive operations, one-time project formulations, equipment-heavy businesses, pure distributors, highly commoditized products, and firms whose owner-dependent know-how or customer concentration prevents a transferable operation.
e.change_evidence: A firm-by-firm review of the 373 injected LMM candidates, classifying recurring revenue, external-customer share, manufacturing versus distribution, customer concentration, owner dependence, and EBITDA normalization, would materially change eligibility.
caveats: No source measures lens eligibility within the injected LMM population.
The broad residual NAICS definition makes database classification errors and mixed revenue models likely.
The injected firm count is an estimate derived from size-class receipts and an industry margin bridge.

s5.bridge: Gallup measures stated five-year sale or ownership-transfer plans among US employer businesses across all industries, not completed qualifying control transfers among LMM 325998 manufacturers. I center near the 22% employer-owner result and widen upward for specialty-chemical strategic interest and older manufacturing ownership, while allowing downward error because plans fail, transfers can be internal or non-control, and environmental liabilities can impede closings.
s5.rationale: Gallup reports 52.3% of US employer businesses are owned by people at least 55 years old and 22% of employer-business owners planned a sale or transfer within five years. A broad, 373-firm estimated LMM universe should produce meaningful availability, but survey intentions are not transactions and chemical-site diligence, customer concentration, and environmental liabilities may prevent a qualifying transfer.
s5.change_evidence: Ten years of closed control transactions from PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, BizBuySell, and specialty-chemical brokers, tagged to 325998 product lines, EBITDA, ownership age, buyer type, and failed-sale reasons, would materially move the range.
caveats: Gallup is cross-industry and includes employers far below the LMM EBITDA band.
Owner plans may include gifts, internal family transfers, or public offerings that do not meet the qualifying-control definition.
Public-company portfolio divestitures illustrate transfer activity but do not measure LMM probability.

q.rationale: Custom formulations, qualification costs, technical support, performance risk, brands, and proprietary activation or blending know-how can make productivity gains less visible and more retainable. Retention is lower for bid-driven, formula-transparent, private-label, or cost-plus products and at contract renewals where large customers demand sharing. Ingevity describes critical inputs and application expertise, while WD-40's margin expansion alongside sales growth illustrates that differentiated maintenance chemicals can retain pricing and operating gains, though neither source directly measures pass-through.
q.change_evidence: A sample of customer contracts and invoice histories showing cost escalators, open-book or tolling terms, bid cadence, rebates, renewal pricing, gross-margin response to cost changes, and post-improvement price concessions would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The public-company sources have scale and brands that many LMM firms lack.
Retention differs materially among activated carbon, custom process aids, toll blends, commodity preparations, and branded maintenance chemicals.
Demand and implementation effects are excluded here.

d5.bridge: Ingevity, Arq, and WD-40 are selected public producers serving only parts of the heterogeneous 325998 basket, and their revenue and management statements do not directly measure constant-price, constant-quality US LMM demand. I translate recurring consumables, environmental-treatment demand, infrastructure and maintenance end uses, and observed maintenance-product sales growth into a modest real quantity-growth range. The bridge may overstate the category if high-growth water and maintenance niches are unrepresentative, or understate it if PFAS treatment and infrastructure spending accelerate.
d5.rationale: Demand is diversified across maintenance, water and gas treatment, environmental remediation, paving, industrial process aids, automotive emissions, and other consumable formulations. Arq identifies environmental regulation and PFAS treatment as activated-carbon demand drivers; WD-40 reported 6% maintenance-product sales growth in fiscal 2025; and Ingevity shows critical but cyclical and weather-sensitive road, lubricant, adhesive, rubber, and process applications. Product bans, reformulation, vehicle electrification, construction cycles, and customer destocking keep the low case below current volume.
d5.change_evidence: Census product-level shipment quantities and BLS producer-price series for the principal 325998 products, supplemented by EPA compliance-driven activated-carbon forecasts and customer volume commitments by end market, would materially move the estimate.
caveats: Public-company sales mix includes activities that may sit outside 325998 and includes price and geographic effects.
The residual category can change mix even if aggregate shipments grow.
Environmental rules can increase treatment demand while simultaneously eliminating particular chemical formulations.

o.rationale: The remaining year-five basket consists of physical sorbents, lubricants, water-treatment products, process aids, and other preparations that require an accountable entity to source inputs, formulate or activate material, test performance, package, document, and comply with chemical and environmental rules. Customers can self-serve ordering and analytics or vertically integrate simple blends, and software can reduce technical-service effort, but it cannot supply the physical chemistry itself.
o.change_evidence: Interviews with procurement and technical leaders at water utilities, industrial plants, distributors, automotive suppliers, and private-label buyers quantifying insourcing, direct raw-material substitution, and software-only displacement would materially change the range.
caveats: The value is conditional on demand remaining in d5, so banned or eliminated products are not counted twice.
Simple blends are more vulnerable to customer insourcing than engineered activated carbon or qualified performance chemicals.

narrative.executive_view: This residual specialty-chemical category offers a broad pool of repeat consumables businesses, but its heterogeneity is the central underwriting problem. AI can improve commercial, planning, quality, regulatory, and maintenance workflows; it is less likely to replace batch operation, material handling, packaging, and accountable release work.
narrative.ai_impact: Near-term use cases include order and demand forecasting, formulation and specification retrieval, SDS and regulatory-document assistance, batch-record review, quality anomaly detection, machine vision, predictive maintenance, purchasing, and customer-support triage. Legacy controls, sparse labeled data, plant safety, validation, and fragmented product lines limit five-year implementation.
narrative.value_capture: Differentiated performance, customer qualification, proprietary know-how, brands, and technical service support retention. Tolling, open-book contracts, commodity bids, powerful buyers, and frequent rebids share benefits, making contract structure more important than the label 'specialty chemical.'
narrative.firm_availability: The injected population of 373 estimated LMM firms is substantial, and many should sell repeat consumables externally. Eligibility and transferability still require firm-level work because the residual NAICS category can include distribution, one-off projects, owner-dependent formulations, captive production, contaminated sites, and businesses with severe customer concentration.
narrative.demand_durability: The basket benefits from physical-world maintenance, water and air purification, environmental remediation, infrastructure, and industrial production. Activated carbon and maintenance products show favorable demand signals, while paving cycles, weather, industrial activity, product regulation, electrification, and formulation bans create dispersion and periodic contraction.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest risks are product-mix opacity, environmental liabilities, raw-material volatility, regulatory bans or reformulation, customer qualification loss, end-market cyclicality, and the possibility that apparent manufacturers are mainly distributors. Public evidence comes from larger selected companies and broad chemical occupations rather than the LMM lens.
narrative.principal_driver: Recurring consumable chemistry across many physical end markets supports repeat demand and selective AI-enabled workflow improvement.
narrative.principal_weakness: A residual NAICS category this heterogeneous can hide low-quality, regulated, commoditized, or non-transferable businesses behind attractive aggregate data.

sources.title: U.S. Census Bureau NAICS 325998 industry definition
sources.supports: Defines the residual miscellaneous chemical product and preparation manufacturing industry and distinguishes it from other named chemical-manufacturing categories.

sources.title: May 2023 National Industry-Specific Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates: NAICS 325 Chemical Manufacturing
sources.supports: Reports production occupations at 39.02% of chemical employment, including chemical-equipment operators at 12.28%, packaging/filling operators at 6.43%, mixing/blending operators at 3.57%, and inspectors/testers at 3.33%.

sources.title: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of Large Language Models
sources.supports: Finds about 15% of US tasks could be completed significantly faster with an LLM alone and 47%-56% with complementary software, without predicting adoption timing.

sources.title: DOE Advanced Manufacturing Office: Thermal Process Intensification Workshop Report
sources.supports: Describes AI and sensors for industrial process control and chemical-manufacturing implementation barriers including application specificity, complex data acquisition, capital intensity, testing, scale-up, disruption, and regulation.

sources.title: Arq, Inc. 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K
sources.supports: Describes activated carbon as a consumable across water, gas, emissions, remediation, food, and industrial markets, with environmental regulation and PFAS treatment as demand drivers.

sources.title: Gallup: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.supports: Reports 52.3% of US employer businesses are owned by people age 55 or older and 22% of employer owners planned a sale or transfer within five years.

sources.title: WD-40 Company Fiscal Year 2025 Results
sources.supports: Reports maintenance-product sales of $590.966 million in fiscal 2025, up 6%, total net sales of $619.985 million, and full-year gross margin of 55.1%.

sources.title: Ingevity Corporation 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K
sources.supports: Describes critical-input uses in roads, lubricants, adhesives, rubber, agrochemicals, water and chemical purification, application expertise, automotive emissions requirements, and weather-sensitive paving demand.
